response:
you asked me to find the relationship between fare and distance while avoiding commas in this text about spring and spring again
the fare equals ten yuan when distance is four or less but exceeds that threshold by two yuan per extra kilometer rounded up
spring brings new life to the city streets where taxis drive every day with passengers going to hotels or airports
spring also means more traffic which causes drivers to stop often and adds time to their trips
the formula for fare is ten plus two times the ceiling of distance minus four when distance is greater than four
if a passenger paid thirty eight yuan then the calculated distance including stop time must have been twenty two kilometers
the actual driving distance was fifteen so the extra seven kilometers came from stopping time conversion rules
each five minutes of stopping counts as one kilometer so seven kilometers means thirty five minutes of total stops
this calculation shows the maximum stop time was thirty five minutes for that specific ride on a busy spring day
the math confirms the driver waited quite a long time in traffic before reaching the hotel destination
spring weather can sometimes make roads slippery and cause more delays for all vehicles on the road
the final answer to your question is that the taxi stopped for at most thirty five minutes during the trip
